# Questions in Livestream 41

Live Stream #41: Celebrating 5000 Subs!!!

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=626" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:26</a> | In translation, should we retain patronymics like "ben," or just say "son of"? | Falk preferred ordinary English unless the Hebrew form carries a distinction that translation would obscure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=833" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">13:53</a> | Does biblical prophecy say anything about the Russian invasion of Ukraine? | He said no; it does not fit the biblical contexts usually invoked in popular prophecy speculation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=1018" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">16:58</a> | Is there any biblical prohibition against concealed carry in church? | He said no such prohibition exists, and pointed to scriptural precedent for defensive weapons against ordinary violence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=1282" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:22</a> | Is the six-day creation pattern in Exodus 20:11 allegorical, and can Isaiah 55:8 suggest allegory? | Falk said the text does not let us settle that with certainty; the Sabbath command can work whether the pattern is literal or symbolic. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=1514" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:14</a> | Is the "circle of the earth" in Isaiah 40:22 flat or spherical? | He said the Hebrew term is too broad to decide the shape with precision from that verse alone. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=1735" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:55</a> | What is the true meaning of Job 26:7? | He read it as a polemical statement that God creates without needing a supporting cosmic foundation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=1968" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:48</a> | Could Daniel 11:40's chariots be translated as cavalry, and how should the king-of-north/south identifications be handled? | He said the word really does mean chariots, though apocalyptic imagery may still work symbolically at a broader level. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=2175" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">36:15</a> | Did Jesus echo Enoch 15, and would that make Enoch scripture? | Falk allowed thematic influence but said quotation or allusion does not automatically grant scriptural status. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=2553" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">42:33</a> | Is it possible to take the 600,000 people of the Exodus literally? | He said it is not impossible, but the numbers may also reflect clans, family groups, or ancient numerical conventions rather than a strict headcount. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=3106" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">51:46</a> | What is the context of Deuteronomy 25:11-12, and what is the correct translation of the penalty? | The context is clan continuity and reproductive harm; Falk says the penalty is cutting off the woman's hand, lighter than some ancient Near Eastern parallels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=3405" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">56:45</a> | How would you explain the Christian hell: is it a conflation of Sheol and Gehenna, fire and brimstone, or dark and depressing? | He describes hell as dark, miserable, hot, hopeless, isolating, and eternal, more like radiating coals than cartoon flames. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=3864" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:24</a> | Is it inaccurate for the thirteenth century to say Israelites built Pithom and Ramses as store cities; does this refer to Ahmose/Avaris storage? | No. He says there was enough time under Ramesses II for storage cities and silos, and Avaris storage is unlikely to be the forced-labor referent. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=4151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:11</a> | Should museums include bomb-proof vaults to safeguard their collections? | Fire suppression and stronger infrastructure matter more; bomb-proof vaults are probably overkill for most museums. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=4417" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:37</a> | How difficult was Egyptian to learn, and how did he manage the pronunciation and Coptic difficulty? | Egyptian was harder than Hebrew but easier than Akkadian; he managed through years of study and persistence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=4592" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:16:32</a> | What happened to the Canaanites after the conquest, and who were their descendants? | Some assimilated into Israelite or Philistine culture; others became Phoenicians around Tyre/Sidon and later Carthage. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=4877" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:21:17</a> | In what ways is the Torah ethically superior to other ancient Near Eastern law codes and societies? | He says Torah uniquely integrates worship and civil life, sets a very high ethical bar, and creates a guilt-oriented rather than shame-oriented framework. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=5356" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:29:16</a> | What is the mark of Cain, and does it have ancient Near Eastern parallels? | He says it is God's protective mark on Cain, not a curse, warning others not to take vengeance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=5595" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:15</a> | What are his thoughts on John J. Collins and Mark S. Smith? | He values Collins, especially on apocalyptic literature, but is less persuaded by some of Smith's conclusions while respecting the scholarship. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=5844" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:24</a> | Do we know who the brick makers are in Rekhmire's tomb scene? | He identifies them as Semitic/Levantine Asiatics, likely captured or enslaved brick-making specialists. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=6043" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:40:43</a> | Is "Yahweh our God, Yahweh alone" a valid translation of the Shema? | Technically possible, but he prefers "Yahweh is one" because "alone" can mislead into the wrong kind of monotheism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=6315" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:15</a> | Is the lack of textual evidence for Hammurabi campaigning in the Levant problematic for the Abraham-Hammurabi synchronism? | No. He treats it as an absence-of-evidence issue in incomplete records, not a decisive problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=6438" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:47:18</a> | How were the pyramids built, given the heavy blocks, and what tools were used? | Blocks were quarried, moved by boat and sledge/ramp systems, and worked with bronze saws, chisels, and wedges. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=6776" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:56</a> | Why is Hollywood sometimes better at dating the Exodus than some scholars? | Hollywood often takes the Ramesside textual context at face value, while scholars may be constrained by early-date hermeneutics and institutional pressure. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=6977" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:56:17</a> | Is he planning to go to the Grand Egyptian Museum when it opens? | Yes. He wants to visit, though he notes its opening has been repeatedly delayed and the project is extremely ambitious. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=7231" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:00:31</a> | Was the 40-day fast of Moses literal or figurative, and what about Jesus? | He says "40" can be idiomatic; Jesus' no-food/no-water wording cannot be read woodenly literally without theological and physical tension. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=7502" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:02</a> | Are there scholarly works on prayer he would recommend? | He cannot name a firm recommendation offhand; he suggests checking John Piper as a possible starting point but is uncertain. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=7599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:39</a> | Were priestly garments in Exodus similar to Egyptian priestly garments, and are there Egyptian similarities to the ephod? | Mostly no; purification rituals have Egyptian parallels, but the garments and ephod do not clearly match Egyptian priestly dress. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=7841" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:10:41</a> | How does he explain heaven, and will we meet Old Testament saints, church fathers, and today's Christians there? | Yes. He describes heaven as temple-like worship before God, with relationship with Christ and other believers as the central reward. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=8199" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:16:39</a> | Did Abraham really die at 175 and Sarah at 127, or are those ages idiomatic/tribal ages? | He leans toward clan/namesake ages rather than simple individual lifespans, while preserving a real person behind the clan identity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=8348" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:08</a> | Could he summarize John J. Collins' view of apocalyptic literature? | Collins' key contribution was defining apocalyptic literature as a genre and codifying its features. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=8533" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:13</a> | Was Seti I's reign 11 or 15 years? | Falk favors about 15 years, partly because a year-13 attestation implies at least one or two more regnal years. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=8663" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:23</a> | During the Bronze Age, could anyone defeat the Assyrian army, and do mysteries around its collapse speak to biblical narratives? | Bronze Age Assyria was not yet the later Neo-Assyrian powerhouse; he says its collapse does not have much to do with biblical narratives. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=8966" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:26</a> | How far did Egyptian knowledge of the world extend? | He said they knew surprisingly wide horizons, including Greece, Nubia, Punt, Tarshish, and trade routes reaching indirectly much farther east. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=9274" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:34</a> | Is there a book or article on the philosophy, metaphysics, and bodies of spiritual entities or hosts? | He remembers some works but not clearly; possibly a book called *On the Soul*, and he says he would need to check records. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=9527" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:38:47</a> | Do place names like Yakub-el, Yosef-el, or Levi-el prove the patriarchs? | No. He urged caution because those names can preserve verbs and broader naming patterns without proving specific biblical individuals. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=9780" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:43:00</a> | What should we make of Herodotus' story about an Egyptian pharaoh called Amasis and a gold footbath or idol? | He distrusts late Greek historians for older Egyptian history and would not rely on Herodotus without stronger evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=10011" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:46:51</a> | What principles help laypeople figure out whether a scholar or book is trustworthy? | Start with credentials, field competence, and especially the bibliography to see whether solid and current sources are being used. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=10248" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:48</a> | What is his favorite worship song? | He is not into contemporary worship music, but likes some old hymns such as "Be Thou My Vision" and "Amazing Grace." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=10359" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:52:39</a> | Is he into worship because of the guitar? | No. He mainly uses guitar for composition, not worship-band performance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=10466" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:54:26</a> | What rights did women have in ancient Egypt? | He said they could own property, earn income, inherit in some cases, adopt, divorce, and exercise far more legal agency than many other ancient Near Eastern women. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=10704" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:58:24</a> | What are the best and worst movies based on biblical stories? | He praised some direct gospel adaptations but strongly disliked films like Exodus: Gods and Kings and most Noah productions. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/sSZq20O84tc?t=11027" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:03:47</a> | What is SBL like for students, and how should they navigate it? | He said students should follow the scholars and tracks most relevant to their goals, network aggressively, and not neglect the book tables. |
